Intro
This is my first review on steam. Well we all start at some point ... :-)
Btw, English is not my mother tongue (but German), but to have a global reachout I decided not to do "German first".
I bought SH5 nearly a week ago and after about 10-12 hrs of playing I am already bored and disappointed.

First some pros:
Good atmospherte overall. The languages in speaking and subtitlles (German/English ) do not match always exactly, but its good enough for me. The U-Boat performs as I was used to it in SH3 and the different settings allow for pro players (doing the math) and amateur Captains as well (simply aim the target, give some orders). 
A good feature was done by assigning points to the crew. Guess that pays off in later missions.

Now some cons:
There is an intro but overall you are guessing what to do. Not the navigation or attack, but the standard navy procedures.
When to use the radio? What orders shall be given in which order to do it properly?
As I did not serve the navy (doing my time in the army) I am not familiar with that but basic military knowledge tells me that there are some procedures for every step you do :-)
The missions are quite strange. (I admit I stopped early, so maybe I miss something here). You are ordered to sink specific tonnage or ships but even if you do so in many cases I wondered why it did not count. 
I ended up ignoring the mission orders and went on hunting as I seemed appropriate.
Also strange that you need to walk to each station to give orders yourself. I expected that you should be able to give orders more easy.
And finally - although most ships do show the battle scars after being hit by torpedo or gunfire, the big ones dont (transporters, carriers), or I simply have a bad hardware.

Overall I would not recommend to buy that game now. Maybe some bugs might get fixed in the short term, but as long as mission controls and order controls are not clear this is justr giving you frustration.

Wow I'm going to have to fight hard here but I have a sincere review that reveals that actual enormous VALUE this game adds to the series (if it was introduceable enough) I will.

I will say one thing about this game. Fighting and traveling are PERFECT IN THIS GAME. They are beyond, without a doubt, the most perfect controls for not only point and click traveling with time dilation adventure, but also precision like freaking Nazi astronauts. Fighting is actually BETTER than Silent Hunter Four, and I know I'll get flak for that, but for how in depth and satisfying with how the controls are in this game, without a doubt these are the most intuitive feeling controls with a torpedo submarine is "shoot it straight and then a little right and left" is such a perfectly simple control movement with this game. They must have poured 99% of their time into that. Even if it's not perfect pistons and sheers, it still works and requires judgement and skill.

What is WEIRD is the walking, and let me go beyond that, who the fuck is this german guy waking you up in the very beginning? This guy sees your eyes are open and just keeps himself in your face for too long asking if you're awake, like the fuck man? Then he skips down the corridor and uncomfortably massages the backs of people he sees, and I'm going to be honest it was fine, but the lack of body animations the rest of the crew had, it was a pretty bad scene. Going beyond that, the interior play of the game is a cool attempt and does something new but does also fail to really add any play value and actually breaks the game's flow up a little. 

I will say now, if I could just 'edit' the flow of the game from start to finish it's actually a 9.5 huge contribution to its genre /10. It performs leaps and bounds which this game has really done for itself. If one read a novel like Operation Drumbeat: U-Boat 123, this is the perfect game to have modding capabilities, or really just altered start dates to feel the soldiery of a sailor in African Germany, and that's where I'd spend my effort if the company actually hired me. Again, great game, too old to fix (maybe), good potential on many things except scary weird start of the game.
This can be such a fun sub simulator to play shame it crashes a lot and has many bugs,  unfortunately they don't fix it and still sell it knowing it has many issues.

(you can use a skill of your hydrophone guy... but since the enemy only shows up for like a second you have to lock, pause the game, do the math and then quickly unpause and fire...) without manual tdc is weird and limited... certain campaigns like the one in the mediterean sea is so full of planes that gets super boring.... and other campaigns is almost impossible to find the target to finish the mission... the carriers in the black pit campaign never showed up... not sure if it was the bug from drm or if they are really that hard to find... all the warship groups had other ships except carriers.

This game is good only, and only, because of our big fat imagination. It is extremely barebones and even the coding is half baked. Save & Load issues, inconsistencies, major bugs (game-breaking, yes I mean it) and unfinished features (wolfpack system is a royal joke). 

I have tried getting back at this game every 2 or 3 years only to stop playing at the exact same spot because that is as much as I could tolerate... It is bad, really bad.

Almost over a decade, yet no fixes.

In my last playthrough however, I did install tons of fixes and mods to make it not just a decent game, but a really good one too yet it means the people who selflessly spent hours after hours upon making this rusty wreck into a true wolf of the deeps deserve the real praise, not this game, not in this state. Hence, the negative review.

If you are going to mod this game out of the atmosphere (subsim forums is the place to go btw.) then by all means get it, remember, you need to be patient and careful with the modding.

If you are looking for a game that you can play without any hassle and problems, just go on and click on that "Not interested" button for this one. You can thank me later for saving your ever-precious time.

Wasserbomben! Oh my god people are wrong about this game and it sucked me in like hull damage in 150m below the sea.

There's a mod called "the Wolves of Steel", requires you to patch the game, update a bunch of files, and be patient while it loads. After that, it's pretty much the greatest game ever (involving submarines) after these overhaul and improvements. 

You can walk around your ship, talk to the dudes that does the hydrophone, radar, weapons guy, navigator and ask "how's your wife" and you boost his morale. Or do it via shortcut on the bottom of the screen. Then you can set sail and sink some ships. 

You can set the realism setting to however you feel like it, whether you want to calculate the firing solutions yourself, or have AOE of the detection ranges of destroyers on the map at all times, this mod enables you the option to do so. They got all the tools you need, several different compasses, protractors, rulers, a chart for sine and cosine.

Everything makes sense logically and a lot of things are simulated in this game, like weather 
 and (sun/moon/wave/broaching/wake/radio/subterfuge/sound/heat/thermals/sonar/bullet drop/hole physics/duds) and it gives reactions from the ships that's monitoring you (or not) a proper logical and realistic reaction to a torpedo fired at them.

But there's some unrealistic things like you can't send radio if your ship isn't fully surfaced, so you must surface fully to send radio reports and hope in these minutes of blowing the tanks, some bombers don't creep up on you. Everything else works fine through the power of mods, although if you want to observe from the periscope depth to add live blips on the map I believe the only person that can do it is you, the player.

And the best thing about all of this is the historical accuracy of the tools it gives you to enact that accuracy, all the maps, trading, mining, air recon, data are real. And the tools, charts, radio signals, orders, and the enigma machine are present and functioning in the game the same way they would in real life. I had to take about 10 minutes to learn how to use the enigma machine they had in ww2 to decipher a message because you get two of those messages even if you turned the encryption off.

If you want a submarine game this is *the one*, looks good, plays good, "Wolf of Steel" mod fixed the bugs. Only complaint is multiplayer is coop only and you can't uboat with friends on the same ship.

PS. You can receive reports of sinking ships to rescue their rafts from both sides of the war, but I always flak gun them because I'm afraid they'll snitch about the uboats to their ally destroyers and then the ocean will be covered with sonar pings and wasserbomben.

PSS. This entire game might feel like it's undertranslated due to the hefty amount of german words you will see. But watch das boot once and it'll get you in the mood.
